name: Temporal
summary: The abstract core block that realizes the Temporal Intent (date/time selection) — the shared machinery every concrete picker pins down. Native-first by construction: a temporal control IS a native date/time input (<input type="date|time|datetime-local">), so the core's job is only the enhancements native cannot express alone — a spatial/linear presentation surface (calendar grid, clock, scroll wheel), locale-aware parsing and display, and range/multi coordination — all behind one intent contract. It composes the Temporal Intent (presentation × granularity), the Locale Intent (parse/format), the Input Intent (pointer vs keyboard vs touch modality), and Focus Delegation (roving the surface). It ships no concrete picker itself: named shallow presets (date-picker, time-picker, datetime-picker, date-range-picker) pin granularity/presentation and bind their native anchor over this one core.
designDecisions: One abstract core + named shallow presets, not re-implementations (#713 option C, ratified 2026-06-15). The date/time/range pickers share one machinery contract — calendar/clock rendering, locale parse/format, keyboard roving, range coordination — so that lives in a single abstract `temporal` core, and each concrete picker is a shallow preset that pins presentation/granularity and binds its native anchor over the core. This mirrors the Selection-Intent family (one substrate, members are distinct trait selections), not a per-picker rewrite.

Native input is the anchor and baseline — enhance, don't replace. A temporal control IS <input type="date">/<time>/<datetime-local>: focusability, form participation, keyboard entry, and a platform fallback all come free, exactly as the slider precedent (the slider block) defaults to <input type="range"> and only swaps to a custom track when native is ruled out. The core defaults to the native input and renders a custom presentation surface (media/linear) only when the paradigm demands it; the `input` presentation is the zero-JS native path.

Three distinct native anchors are the concrete reason for named presets — unlike the single-anchor slider. The slider had one native anchor (input[type=range]) so it needed no named members; the temporal family spans THREE platform anchors (date, time, datetime-local), each with its own parse/format and value shape, which is the platform-level reason date-picker / time-picker / datetime-picker exist as named presets rather than props on one element (#713 C; the slider precedent lacked this and so stayed a single block).

presentation × granularity is the whole dimension space (from the Temporal Intent). presentation is one of media (spatial: calendar grid, analog clock), linear (sequential: scroll wheel, listbox), or input (text: masked/native); granularity is one of point, range, or multi. A preset is exactly a pin of these two dimensions over the core; the core itself pins neither (it is the abstract contract). Locale-aware formatting flows through the Locale Intent and is published on the native input's value plus an accessible formatted label, the same split the slider uses (aria-valuetext formatted, aria-valuenow raw).

ariaApgKeyboard.usage: The abstract date/time core: native-first over `<input type="date|time|datetime-local">`, falling back to a `role="dialog"` + `role="grid"` calendar with the APG date-picker keyboard model.
